title: UMATILLA COUNTY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM - 211info
text: UMATILLA COUNTY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES UMATILLA COUNTY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a wide range of services that may include networking and outreach, facilitation of Individual Support Plan (ISP) meetings, assisting in the referral process to community service agencies for supports, providing support to individuals and families in a variety of planning meetings, assisting in finding residential placement and employment services, as well as referrals to support services brokerages. The DD program staff may also review complaints of abuse and immediately take steps to assess further risk. In some cases, protective services may be offered to avoid further harm. 817 SE 13th Street Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Must have the all of the following: -A disability that originated in the brain -The disability must be established prior the age of 22, or in the case of intellectual disability the condition must be determined before 18 years of age. -Must result in significant impairments in at least two of the following areas of daily function: Self-care, communication, cognition, mobility, self direction, capacity of independent living, and/or economic self sufficiency -The impairments must not be primarily related to: substance abuse, mental illness, an emotional disorder, ADHD, a learning disability, or sensory impairment. Impairments must be expected to last indefinitely. Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm, Friday 8am-noon Email (541) 278-4450 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 278-4450 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and to schedule an appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, Oregon Health Plan (OHP), if applicable LANGUAGES: English, Spanish interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Umatilla County Services: Case/Care Management * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Case/Care Management * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Home/Community Based Developmental Disabilities Programs Residential Placement Services for People with Disabilities If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
